zh-CN.title: 反馈场景
zh-CN.typical_response: 收到评价或修改意见时，你可能先捕捉语气和关系信号，再处理信息本身。
zh-CN.strength: 能注意到反馈背后的真实意图和关系温度。
zh-CN.cost: 如果恢复不足，容易把反馈体验成否定。
zh-CN.better_move: 先拆分事实、情绪和下一步：对方说了什么，我有什么感受，我要确认什么。

en.title: Feedback
en.typical_response: When receiving critique or edits, you may notice tone and relational cues before processing the information.
en.strength: You can notice intent and relational temperature behind feedback.
en.cost: When recovery is weak, feedback may feel like rejection.
en.better_move: Separate facts, feeling, and next step: what was said, what I feel, what I need to clarify.

zh-CN.title: 冲突场景
zh-CN.typical_response: 出现分歧时，你可能在维护关系和表达自己之间摇摆。
zh-CN.strength: 有机会同时看见双方需求。
zh-CN.cost: 如果没有暂停流程，容易过度解释、回避或急于修复。
zh-CN.better_move: 先确认共同目标，再只讨论一个具体问题。

en.title: Conflict
en.typical_response: During disagreement, you may move between preserving the relationship and expressing yourself.
en.strength: You can often see needs on both sides.
en.cost: Without a pause process, you may over-explain, avoid, or rush repair.
en.better_move: Name the shared goal first, then discuss one specific issue.

zh-CN.title: 关系边界
zh-CN.typical_response: 当别人需要你支持时，你可能很快进入理解或承担。
zh-CN.strength: 容易让对方感到被接住。
zh-CN.cost: 支持和代替之间的边界可能变模糊。
zh-CN.better_move: 先问自己：我能支持到哪里，哪一部分仍然属于对方。

en.title: Relationship Boundaries
en.typical_response: When someone needs support, you may quickly move into understanding or taking responsibility.
en.strength: Others may feel received and understood.
en.cost: The line between supporting and replacing can blur.
en.better_move: Ask yourself: how far can I support, and which part remains theirs?

zh-CN.title: 团队协作
zh-CN.typical_response: 在多人协作中，你可能同时追踪任务、关系和现场情绪。
zh-CN.strength: 能发现协作中的隐性阻力。
zh-CN.cost: 如果总是主动协调，容易承担过多情绪劳动。
zh-CN.better_move: 把观察转成具体协作请求，而不是默默补位。

en.title: Team Collaboration
en.typical_response: In group work, you may track task, relationship, and emotional climate at the same time.
en.strength: You can notice hidden friction in collaboration.
en.cost: If you always coordinate, you may carry too much emotional labor.
en.better_move: Turn observations into concrete collaboration requests instead of silently compensating.

zh-CN.title: 压力恢复
zh-CN.typical_response: 高压后你可能仍在脑内回放对话、评价或细节。
zh-CN.strength: 复盘能力可以帮助你学习模式。
zh-CN.cost: 没有恢复边界时，复盘会变成反刍。
zh-CN.better_move: 给复盘设限：只写三个事实、一个感受、一个下次动作。

en.title: Pressure Recovery
en.typical_response: After pressure, you may replay conversations, judgments, or details in your head.
en.strength: Review can help you learn patterns.
en.cost: Without recovery boundaries, review turns into rumination.
en.better_move: Limit review to three facts, one feeling, and one next action.

zh-CN.title: 职业环境
zh-CN.typical_response: 你对岗位中的人际密度、情绪劳动和反馈强度会有不同消耗。
zh-CN.strength: 理解环境变量后，更容易选择适合自己的工作节奏。
zh-CN.cost: 只看职位名称，容易忽略真正消耗你的工作条件。
zh-CN.better_move: 评估岗位时优先询问互动频率、冲突频率和恢复空间。

en.title: Career Environment
en.typical_response: Interpersonal density, emotional labor, and feedback intensity can drain you differently across roles.
en.strength: Understanding environmental variables helps you choose a better work rhythm.
en.cost: Job titles alone can hide the conditions that actually drain you.
en.better_move: When evaluating roles, ask about interaction frequency, conflict frequency, and recovery space.
